summary.business_type: Document-photo / PDF / signature utility SaaS (India-first, free, in-browser/no-upload)
top_findings: Corrected prior baseline error: 'restore Disallow: /_next/' recommendation was wrong — that removal (2026-06-26) was a deliberate, correct fix for render-based indexing, not a regression.

Page-type framing gap on /exam-requirements/{exam}/: SERP consensus for '{exam} photo resizer' is ~90% Tool/Interactive; page title/H1 still read spec-first ('Photo Size') even after embedding the actual resizer tool. SXO verdict moved CRITICAL -> MEDIUM, not ALIGNED.
Zero outbound government-portal citation links on all 3 recent/new blog posts despite each claiming verification against an official source.
/tools/sign-image/ has zero images despite accelerating GSC demand (257 impressions, up from ~120) for 'sign on image'.
12 of 19 site-wide raster images remain unconverted PNGs (500-820 KB); hero/LCP fix was scoped narrowly and didn't extend to the rest of the inventory.
quick_wins: Add 1-2 outbound government-portal links per recent blog post (ECI/NSDL/Sarathi)
Add back-links from pan-card-photo-size and driving-licence-photo-size-sarathi to the Government ID pillar
Register a govId cluster entry in lib/blog.ts CLUSTERS map
Convert the remaining 12 oversized PNGs to WebP

auditor: Claude — 5 parallel specialist re-audits (Technical, Content/E-E-A-T, SXO, Cluster, Visual) + direct live verification of every finding

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: All 3 redirects (www->apex, http->https, legacy voter-id URL) verified live, 301, single-hop
Sitemap 215/215 URLs return 200, trailing-slash/HTTPS/lowercase consistent, duplicate tiers correctly excluded
howToSchema() null-emission bug fixed; BlogPosting.image now per-post; WebSite.SearchAction present
SSR/SSG confirmed: full content in raw HTML, no client-only rendering dependency

findings.title: Prior baseline's 'restore /_next/ disallow' recommendation is incorrect
findings.description: git log -p app/robots.ts confirms the disallow was deliberately removed 2026-06-26 with correct reasoning: Google needs CSS/JS access to render pages; blocking /_next/ can hurt render-based indexing and mobile-friendliness evaluation.
findings.recommendation: No action. Do not re-add the disallow. Baseline (2026-06-23) simply predates this correct fix.

findings.title: IndexNow not implemented
findings.recommendation: Implement IndexNow key file for faster Bing/Yandex indexing of new content.

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: Government ID pillar: 1,601 words, 10 H2s, 4-portal comparison table, FAQ schema, real freshness gap (published 06-25, modified 07-08)
Voter ID spoke refresh shows genuine freshness signal (not same-day stamping)
Exam-requirement sample pages clear word-count floors even after resizer embed (voter-id 634w, ssc 636w, driving-licence 686w)
Consistent author/reviewer schema across all recent content

findings.title: how-to-sign-on-image-online is thin for a blog post
findings.description: 661 words vs. the 1,500-word blog minimum; each of 7 H2 sections is 1-3 sentences with no stats or case examples.
findings.recommendation: Expand with a troubleshooting section or reclassify as a tool-support how-to (300+ word minimum, already clears that).

findings.title: Zero outbound government-portal citation links
findings.description: No href to any .gov.in/nic.in/nsdl.com/utiitsl.com/eci.gov.in domain found in rendered HTML of the 3 recent/new posts, despite text claiming 'verified against the official source'.
findings.recommendation: Add 1-2 direct outbound links per post to the specific portal cited.

findings.title: Author jobTitle undersells research/verification angle
findings.description: 'easyPhoto developer & document-spec researcher' propagated to 3 more pages, same dilution flagged in the 06-23 baseline.
findings.recommendation: Rename to emphasize research/verification over development.

categories.name: On-Page SEO
what_works: Titles/meta/canonicals/JSON-LD present on all sampled pages

findings.title: Score reflects a template-level title-length check plus live GSC field data, not a regression
findings.description: Down from 67 (06-23 baseline) because this pass ran a check the baseline didn't (title overflow across the exam-page pattern) and confirmed a page-specific CTR failure with real field data.
findings.recommendation: Carried forward from the 07-02 on-page refresh; see that findings file for the specific overflow cases.

categories.name: Schema
what_works: 4 JSON-LD blocks confirmed live today on every sampled page (homepage, sign-image, voter-id, both new blog posts)

findings.title: No deep schema validation run this cycle
findings.description: Only live presence was re-verified; the 62/100 score is carried forward from the 2026-06-18 baseline.
findings.recommendation: Run a full schema validation pass (Rich Results Test / schema.org validator) on the next audit cycle.

categories.name: Performance
what_works: LCP 1,881ms (Good), CLS 0.00 (Good), INP 125ms (Good) — all 3 ranking Core Web Vitals pass on real CrUX field data
Hero image converted to WebP with fetchPriority=high + preload, addressing the prior Critical mobile-LCP finding at the source level

findings.title: FCP and TTFB are marginally over Google's threshold
findings.description: FCP 1,822ms (22ms over the 1,800ms Good threshold); TTFB 864ms (64ms over the 800ms Good threshold). Neither is a ranking Core Web Vital.
findings.recommendation: Monitor; not urgent given both are barely over and the ranking trio all pass.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness
what_works: GPTBot, OAI-SearchBot, ChatGPT-User, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot all explicitly allowed in robots.txt, re-verified live

findings.title: No deep passage-citability re-audit run this cycle
findings.description: Score carried forward from 2026-06-23 baseline; only crawler access was re-verified live.
findings.recommendation: Run a full GEO re-audit (passage-level citability, entity clarity) on the next cycle.

categories.name: Images
what_works: Hero/LCP image pair fixed: WebP, confirmed via CrUX field data
Alt text and CLS-prevention patterns solid site-wide

findings.title: 12 of 19 referenced raster images still unconverted PNGs
findings.description: 500-820 KB each, including one homepage section image and 7 across blog posts. The hero fix was scoped narrowly to the LCP-critical pair and didn't extend to the rest of the inventory.
findings.recommendation: Convert remaining PNGs to WebP/AVIF, prioritizing the homepage section image and highest-traffic blog posts.

sxo_gap_score.note: Separate from the SEO Health Score per SXO methodology. Up from 41 (exam cluster only, 06-23 baseline).

pages.verdict: MEDIUM mismatch (down from CRITICAL) — tool now embedded but title/H1 still spec-first vs. SERP's tool-first consensus
ALIGNED page type; execution-depth gap (zero images, 296 words)

cluster_health.note: Up from ~5.5. Government ID pillar/spoke relationship is clean (verified: no cannibalization). Two structural gaps found: 2 of 3 spokes missing mandatory back-link to pillar (verified), and the two passport pillar posts have zero cross-links and restate identical specs (verified, worse than baseline).

phases.name: Phase 1: Critical Fixes
items: Add outbound government-portal citation links to the 3 recent blog posts
Add back-links from pan-card-photo-size and driving-licence-photo-size-sarathi to the Government ID pillar
Register a govId entry in lib/blog.ts CLUSTERS map
Convert remaining 12 unconverted PNGs to WebP

phases.name: Phase 2: High-Impact Improvements
items: Test voter-id title-vs-intent change on ONE page (not template-wide) and watch GSC 2-3 weeks before any wider rollout
Decide: expand how-to-sign-on-image-online to blog depth, or reclassify as a lighter tool-support page

phases.name: Phase 3: Content & Authority
items: Build /exam-requirements/aadhaar/ for pattern parity with the other 3 Gov-ID documents
Off-page/backlinks: still Tier 0 (no Moz key) — the biggest lever outside code, not actionable from this repo

phases.name: Phase 4: Monitoring & Iteration
items: Re-pull GSC 28-day data and refresh KEYWORD-MAP-2026-07.md after each deploy
Watch the voter-id title test specifically for 2-3 weeks
Re-run CrUX pull monthly for a CWV trend line
